_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of criteria for evaluating collection products.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of product management practices as applied to data products, including roadmap prioritization, consumer discovery, feedback loops, and value measurement.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-K-016 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-008 (OP, cosine: 0.465), AI-A-002 (AI, cosine: 0.455).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.3/10 (raw 0.434). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, products]. DCWF-only terms [collection, criteria, evaluating] vs WIDAI-only terms [applied, consumer, data, discovery, feedback, loops] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.890, R=0.850). P exceeds R by 0.040 — the DCWF element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.9%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of criteria for evaluating collection products." and "Knowledge of product management practices as applied to data products, including". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.505 > STS 0.434 (gap +0.071). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, products] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, products]. DCWF-only: [collection, criteria, evaluating]. WIDAI-only: [applied, consumer, data, discovery, feedback, loops].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.9% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
